paper_title: Pine Reproductive Cycle and Data Collection in Boulder County

methods.method_name: Pollen and Seed Cone Production Monitoring
methods.description: This method involves monitoring and collecting data on pollen and seed cone production from ponderosa pine populations across different elevations in Boulder County, Colorado. The purpose is to understand the reproductive cycle and variability in gamete production across different environmental conditions.

steps.description: Monitor pollen and seed cone production from seven populations in the foothills of Boulder County, Colorado, spanning an elevational range of 1,700-3,050 m.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen and seed cone production data from multiple pine populations

steps.description: Obtain seed production data from Shepperd et al. (2006) for the Manitou Experimental Forest in Woodland Park, Colorado at an elevation of 2,500 m.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve seed production data from existing study

steps.description: Count the number of branch tips on a tree with male strobili each June to quantify pollen production.
steps.searchable_summary: count branch tips with male strobili for pollen production

steps.description: Count individual seed cones on each tree between July and October.
steps.searchable_summary: count seed cones on trees for seed production

steps.description: Ensure continuity and consistency of data collection by having one or more authors present for all counts.
steps.searchable_summary: validate data collection consistency with author presence
